summary: Matched the 96-byte common fighter demo callback function `ftCo_800C6150` by moving it out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s` into a new C translation unit, `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DemoCallback0.c`. The function changes the fighter motion state to `ftCo_MS_Rebirth` with `Ft_MF_None`, then sets two packed Fighter flags, `x2219_b2` and `x2219_b1`, to true. The PR also added a dedicated header, updated build object ordering, removed the old prototype from `ft_0C31.h`, and included the new header from `ftdemo.c`.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementation of `ftCo_800C6150` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s`.
Removed the local `.sdata2` constants `ftCo_804D8DC0` and `ftCo_804D8DC4` from the assembly file; the C call expresses the same arguments as `0`, `1`, and `0`.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DemoCallback0.c` containing a matching C implementation of `ftCo_800C6150`.
Added the new C object to `TEXT_O_FILES` in `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ft_0C31.s.o` assembly object.
Moved the prototype for `ftCo_800C6150` out of the broad `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h` header.
smart_moves: Kept the newly matched function in a focused ftCommon source file named after the callback area instead of leaving it in the catch-all `ft_0C31` assembly/header grouping.
Used existing typed aliases and helpers: `ftCo_GObj*`, `Fighter*`, and `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` rather than manual offset access.
Expressed the two `rlwimi` byte bit writes as direct packed bitfield assignments, `fp->x2219_b2 = true` and `fp->x2219_b1 = true`, making the assembly intent clear.
Used named motion constants, `ftCo_MS_Rebirth` and `Ft_MF_None`, instead of raw literals like motion state `0xc` and flags `0`.

Preserved build ordering by adding the C object immediately before the residual `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s.o`, allowing only the matched function to be removed from assembly while the rest of the assembly file remained in place.
decomp_lessons: Small 96-byte asm functions can often be matched cleanly by translating the call signature and packed flag writes into existing fighter abstractions.

When splitting one function out of a monolithic assembly file, remove only that function and any now-local constants it consumed, then add the C object before the remaining assembly object in `obj_files.mk`.

A `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` sequence with assembly arguments `r4 = 0xc`, `r5 = 0`, `r6 = 0`, `f1 = 0`, `f2 = 1`, `f3 = 0`, and final pointer/null argument can map cleanly to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, ftCo_MS_Rebirth, Ft_MF_None, 0, 1, 0, NULL)`.
Byte bit insert instructions targeting Fighter offset `0x2219` can correspond to named bitfields such as `x2219_b2` and `x2219_b1`; prefer the named fields when they already exist.
After creating a dedicated header for a newly matched common fighter file, update direct users like `ftdemo.c` and remove the declaration from older placeholder aggregate headers.
naming_conventions: New ftCommon files follow the pattern `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_<Area>.c` and `.h`; this PR introduced `ftCo_DemoCallback0.c` and `ftCo_DemoCallback0.h`.
The matched function retained its address-derived symbol name `ftCo_800C6150` because no more descriptive function name was established in the evidence.
The new header guard used `GALE01_0C6150`, matching the address-oriented style visible in the diff.
Header declarations preserve address comments, e.g. `/* 0C6150 */ void ftCo_800C6150(ftCo_GObj* gobj);`.
Common fighter motion and flag constants should use established names such as `ftCo_MS_Rebirth` and `Ft_MF_None` rather than raw numeric values.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched the `lwz r31, 0x2c(r3)` fighter data load with `Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj);`.
Matched the call to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` by selecting the correct named motion state and preserving float argument order as `0, 1, 0` plus trailing `NULL`.
Translated consecutive `rlwimi` updates to the same byte at offset `0x2219` into two boolean bitfield assignments.
Removed the original assembly label `ftCo_800C6150` after adding the equivalent C object to avoid duplicate symbols.
Removed obsolete `.sdata2` constants used only by the deleted function, reducing residual assembly data.
follow_up_queries: Find other functions in `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s` that perform simple `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls followed by Fighter flag updates.
Search for remaining direct uses of `x2219_b1` and `x2219_b2` to confirm naming and bitfield semantics.
Audit aggregate headers like `ft_0C31.h` for more declarations that should move into dedicated ftCommon headers as functions are matched.
Look for other demo callback functions that may belong near `ftCo_DemoCallback0.c`.
